title: LATAM Patient Communication Infrastructure
problem: Patient communication in LATAM is fragmented. Phone tag for appointment scheduling. No automated reminders for appointments or preventive care. No digital intake forms. Patients show up unprepared. No post-visit follow-up communication. Language barriers (indigenous populations). Low health literacy complicates communication.
solution: Patient communication platform. Automated SMS/WhatsApp reminders. Digital intake forms (multilingual). Two-way messaging with clinic staff. Post-visit care instructions. Preventive care nudges. Integration with EMR. Patient portal for accessing records and messaging.
technology: WhatsApp Business API, SMS automation, mobile forms, patient portal, EMR integration, multilingual support
target_customer: Primary care practices, hospital outpatient departments, specialty clinics, dental practices
traction_signals: Proven models (Luma Health, Phreesia in US)
regulatory_notes: WhatsApp Business API terms, patient data privacy, medical communication regulations
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Patient communication is essential infrastructure. WhatsApp-first for LATAM. Recurring SaaS revenue. Reduces no-shows = immediate ROI for practices.
notes: BOREALING: Patient communication is basic infrastructure. But in LATAM, WhatsApp is the internet. WhatsApp-first strategy is key differentiator.
